select_when: Performance, learning, or trust depends on giving people information they can act on rather than vague reassurance.
The goal is to improve future behavior while preserving enough relationship quality that the feedback will actually be heard.
A team needs correction that is specific, evidence-backed, and tied to standards rather than personal attack.
An issue will keep repeating unless the discussion moves from the case-at-hand to the underlying machine or process.
avoid_when: Feedback is technically accurate but so poorly timed, context-free, or disrespectful that defensiveness will overwhelm learning.
The moment mainly requires decision clarity, boundary enforcement, or role authority rather than a collaborative learning conversation.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model on specific corrective feedback, relationship-safe delivery, and machine-level improvement rather than generic management rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not widen into broad coaching catalogs because the raw source is strongest on specificity, invitation, and process-level correction.
open_questions: The source spans mastery, radical truth, and AI evaluation examples, but the curated surface stays on first-order human feedback loops.

select_when.note: The strengths and playbook sections explicitly support actionable feedback, future behavior change, standards-based correction, and machine-level discussion.

avoid_when.note: Both avoid conditions come directly from the source warnings about defensive reactions, timing, and relational alienation.

input_type.note: The source activates around performance correction and learning loops, while the compact type label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The playbook yields a structured, actionable feedback loop; the typed output label is a compact reviewed abstraction.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses what went wrong and reasons causally about how process changes alter future behavior.
